subject: Water in my basement after Saturday storm – 412 Granville Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I have lived at 412 Granville Ave for over twenty years and my basement has never taken on water like it did this past Saturday night. At least three inches standing on the floor by Sunday morning. The catch basin at the corner of Granville and 24th is completely blocked with leaves and debris — I could see it overflowing while it was still raining. I am asking that crews inspect and clear that drain as soon as possible. I cannot afford another episode like this.

subject: Water in my basement after Saturday storm – 412 Granville Ave
body_clean: Ms. Ferencz, thank you for reaching out and I am very sorry to hear about the water in your basement. I have forwarded your message to Public Works Director Frank DiMeo and asked that the catch basin at Granville and 24th be inspected this week. Mr. DiMeo's crew will follow up directly. If you experience any further flooding before they arrive, please do not hesitate to call our office.

subject: Flooded intersection at Bohland and 19th – recurring problem
body_clean: Hi Mayor — every heavy rain the corner of Bohland and 19th turns into a pond. After Tuesday's storm it was hub-cap deep and lasted most of the day. School buses had to reroute. I've seen Public Works come by and look at it but nothing has changed. Is there a permanent fix in the pipeline or are we just going to keep sandbagging this problem? I'm at 1904 Bohland and I'm tired of watching kids wade through it.

subject: Flooded intersection at Bohland and 19th – recurring problem
body_clean: Mr. Mwangi — Frank DiMeo, Public Works. That corner is on our radar. The catch basin on the northwest quadrant has a partial collapse on the lateral pipe — documented it last fall. I have a crew scheduled the week of May 18th to rod the line and assess whether we need a full basin replacement. That would go into the fall capital list if the pipe is too far gone. I'll make sure someone knocks on your door before we dig.

subject: Basement flooding – 2119 Harvard Ave – May 9 storm
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I am writing to report that my finished basement at 2119 Harvard Avenue flooded during the May 9th storm. We had about four inches of standing water and lost a water heater and some furniture. My neighbor across the alley had the same problem. There is a catch basin in the alley between Harvard and Morris that looks completely clogged — you can see a mat of leaves packed into it. Please send someone out. I have photos if they would be useful.

subject: Standing water on Frederick Ave near 25th – four days now
body_clean: Mayor, the low spot on Frederick Ave just south of 25th has been underwater for four days. Water is not draining — the inlet grate looks buried under silt. Cars are swerving onto the sidewalk to avoid it. I've got a kid who rides his bike on that street and I'm worried about him hitting water he can't see the bottom of. Can someone from Public Works get out this week? This is lasting longer than usual even for spring.

subject: Standing water on Frederick Ave near 25th – four days now
body_clean: Mr. Orozco — DiMeo here. Had a crew out on Frederick at 25th this afternoon. The inlet grate was packed solid with silt and gravel; cleared it and flow is restored. The pavement depression is a separate issue on the five-year resurfacing list — we'll look at whether we can mill and patch that pocket sooner given the pooling. Water should be gone by tomorrow morning barring more rain. Thank you for flagging it.

subject: Drainage issue near Pember Park playground
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo! Hope you're doing well. I wanted to flag the area near the Pember Park playground — after every rain this spring the whole east side of the park stays soggy for days. Kids can't even use the swings without sinking into mud. My son came home with mud up to his shins last Saturday. I know spring is just wet but this seems worse than past years. Could Parks or Public Works look at whether the grading there can be improved? We love that park!

subject: Drainage issue near Pember Park playground
body_clean: Patrice, thank you as always for keeping us in the loop! I've asked Kevin O'Brien in Parks to walk that section with Frank DiMeo from Public Works this week to evaluate the grading. The east corner of Pember has been flagged before and you may be right that we need to revisit the drainage swale there. One of them will follow up with you directly once they've had a look. Hang in there — hoping for a dry stretch soon!

subject: Third basement flood this spring – 318 Madison St
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, this is the third time since April that my basement at 318 Madison Street has flooded. I am not talking about a little dampness — water is coming through the floor drain and through the foundation wall. I have called Public Works twice. They came out, looked at the catch basin on the corner, and left. Then it floods again. I need to know what is being done structurally to address storm sewer capacity on this block, not just a crew clearing leaves.

subject: Alley behind 2502 Rice Ave completely flooded
body_clean: Good morning Mayor — the alley directly behind 2502 Rice Ave has been a swamp since the storms last week. Water is about six inches deep and not moving. I had to carry groceries over my head to get to my back door. The alley drain cover looks caked over with mud and I am not sure it ever gets cleaned. Is there a schedule for alley drain maintenance or is it all reactive? Happy to show someone around if needed.

subject: Washington Blvd and 50th Ave floods every spring – why no permanent fix?
body_clean: To the Mayor's Office: I have been on Washington Boulevard for seventeen years and every single spring the corner of Washington and 50th Avenue floods to about eight inches and stays that way for two or three days. I know it rains in April. What I do not understand is why the same corner floods to the same depth every year and no one seems to fix the underlying cause. Is this corner in any capital improvement plan? I would appreciate a written response.

subject: Blocked storm drain on Hirsch Ave – two emails ignored
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I have emailed the Village twice about the blocked storm drain on Hirsch Ave in front of 1618 and received nothing back. After the May 14th storm the water was up to my front porch step. I have a diabetic foot wound and I literally cannot wade through that. My neighbor at 1622 has the same problem. I am not asking for anything special, just that someone come clear the drain. Please help.

subject: Ponding in front of 714 Bellwood Ave
body_clean: Hello, I'm writing about the ponding on Bellwood Avenue in front of my house at 714. After last Thursday's rain there was a pond almost to the centerline of the road that lasted until Saturday. The grate in front of my house looks intact but it's surrounded by silt. Is this something Public Works can address with cleaning and maybe some regrading near the gutter? This block does sit low, but this spring has felt worse than previous years. Thank you.

subject: Stormwater running through my garage – 3015 Monroe St
body_clean: Mayor, during the June 3rd storm stormwater ran through the alley, under my garage door, and across the floor — about two inches. This has happened twice this spring. The alley behind Monroe slopes toward my property and there used to be a channel drain at the alley's low point that I think has been silted over for years. I'm going to need to pour a concrete berm if this keeps up, but I wanted to ask the Village first whether this is something you would address. 3015 Monroe St.

subject: Sewage smell in basement after flooding – 209 St. Paul Ave
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I need to report something alarming. After the flooding in our basement last Sunday I noticed a sewage odor that has not gone away. I believe the floor drain backed up with sanitary sewer water, not just storm runoff. The smell is making my family sick. I have a five-year-old in the house. I am at 209 St. Paul Ave. Is this something the Village can inspect? I don't know who to call and I'm genuinely scared about health risks.

subject: Catch basin on Englewood Ave full of debris
body_clean: Hi there — just flagging that the catch basin on the east side of Englewood Ave between 19th and Marshall is full of gunk. I walked by after Tuesday's rain and it was basically a trash pit — leaves, a plastic bag, fast food wrappers. Water was still draining but slowly. If it were cleared it might handle the next storm better. I live nearby at 1912 Englewood. No emergency, just want it on someone's list.

subject: Standing water in back yard – 1107 Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Mayor — 1107 Hirsch Ave. The rear of my yard has been a lake since the June 3rd rain. I'm losing plants and I'm worried my garage foundation is sitting in standing water. The neighbor behind me has a yard that drains toward mine and I've always had a slight water problem, but this spring is the worst I've seen. Is there any stormwater assistance program for homeowners or is this entirely a private-property issue? Just looking for guidance.

subject: Storm drain grate missing on St. Charles near railroad underpass
body_clean: Good afternoon — one of the storm drain grates on St. Charles Road near the railroad underpass is missing. I'm not sure if it washed off or was stolen, but there is just an open hole. I almost stepped in it last night walking to the Metra. This seems like a hazard on top of a drainage problem. I reported it to the non-emergency police line and they told me to contact the Village. Please get this fixed before someone gets hurt.
address_hint: St. Charles Rd near railroad underpass

subject: Storm drain grate missing on St. Charles near railroad underpass
body_clean: Ms. Fuentes — DiMeo, Public Works. A missing grate on a pedestrian route is an emergency work order. Sending a crew this afternoon to barricade the opening and assess the frame. If we have a matching grate in stock it goes in today; if not, we order on an expedited basis and keep the hole barricaded until it arrives. We'll check the adjacent grates on that block while we're out there — that stretch near the underpass takes heavy debris flow.
address_hint: St. Charles Rd near railroad underpass

subject: My block always the last to drain – 2207 Marshall Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I live at 2207 Marshall Avenue and I have a simple question: why does the rest of the neighborhood dry out within a day after rain but my block stays flooded for three or four days? I've lived here since 1981. It was not always this bad. The street has sunk noticeably over the past decade and I think the drains no longer sit at the low point of the road. Is there anyone at the Village who looks at gradual infrastructure change like this? I feel like we are being neglected.

subject: Water coming in under back door – 3304 Washington Blvd rear
body_clean: Hi Mayor — first time emailing the Village. After the June 3rd storm water was coming in under my back door from the yard. The grade behind the house seems to slope toward the building rather than away from it. My landlord says it's the Village's problem; the Village website says it's the homeowner's problem. I'm just the renter caught in the middle with wet floors. Could someone at least come look and tell me who's responsible? 3304 Washington Blvd, rear unit.

subject: Eastern Ave sidewalk flooded – 320 Eastern Ave area
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo — quick note from me again! The sidewalk on Eastern Ave between my house at 320 and the corner of 25th has been flooded or muddy for most of May. I had to push my stroller in the street to get to the park and that doesn't feel safe. The parkway strip there is really compacted and doesn't absorb anything. I'm wondering if planting native grasses or adding a little bioswale there might help long-term — I've seen it work in other towns. But short-term, can someone please clear the drain at 25th and Eastern?

subject: Flooding at 25th and Hirsch – getting worse each year
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo — I live at 2507 Hirsch and the intersection at 25th Avenue floods badly in every significant spring rain. This year is the worst in six years. I took video on my phone if it's useful. The water completely blocks pedestrian access and I've watched cars stall trying to drive through. I'm asking for a proper assessment of storm sewer capacity here, not just leaf clearing. I am a civil engineer by training and happy to share my observations with whoever does the assessment.

subject: Flooding photos – Morris Ave and 22nd Ave intersection
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, I am attaching photos of the corner of Morris Avenue and 22nd Avenue taken the morning after the June 3rd storm. The water reached the lower edge of the stop sign and vehicles were still attempting to drive through. This is a safety hazard. I am asking formally that this intersection be evaluated for a drainage upgrade and that the catch basin be cleaned as a short-term measure. I live at 2208 Morris Ave and have been here eight years. This intersection is always wet, but this spring is beyond anything I have seen.

subject: Neighbor's downspout flooding my yard – 617 Granville Ave
body_clean: Hi Mayor, I don't know if this is something your office handles but my neighbor at 615 Granville has a downspout extension that runs along the fence and dumps directly onto my yard. It wasn't a big deal in dry years but this spring the volume is overwhelming — my backyard has standing water for weeks. Is there an ordinance about where downspout runoff can be directed? And is there someone at the Village I can talk to before I have to hire a lawyer? I'm at 617 Granville Ave.
